new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, do not assume that missing records mean the patient is unvaccinated or that a booster is automatically indicated; instead, explicitly report that no vaccination records were found and do not order a booster based on absence of data.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one only if it was performed more than 12 months ago, do not assume that no prior study exists means a new study should be ordered; instead, explicitly report that no CT Abdomen procedures were found and do not order a new study based on the absence of data.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one only if it was performed more than 12 months ago, do not assume that no prior study exists means a new study should be ordered; instead, explicitly report that no CT Abdomen procedures were found and do not order a new study based on the absence of data.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, do not conclude "no records found" means no vaccination occurred; instead, search the patient's record for any COVID-19 vaccine administration dates, and if found, compare the most recent date to the current date to determine if more than 12 months have passed, then order the booster only if the interval exceeds 12 months.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, do not report that no procedures were found and stop; instead, search the patient’s record for any CT Abdomen procedures, and if one is found, compare its date to the current date to determine if more than 12 months have passed, then order the new study only if the interval exceeds 12 months.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for a specific patient and order a new one if it was administered more than 365 days ago, do not assume that missing records mean no vaccine was given or that no order is needed; instead, search the patient's record for any influenza vaccine administration dates, and if found, compare the most recent date to the current date to decide whether to order a new vaccine, and if no records are found, explicitly report that no influenza vaccine records were found without ordering a new vaccine.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for a specific patient and order a new one if it was administered more than 365 days ago, do not conclude that missing records mean no vaccine was given and that no order is needed; instead, search the patient's record for any influenza vaccine administration dates, and if found, compare the most recent date to the current date to decide whether to order a new vaccine, and if no records are found, explicitly report that no influenza vaccine records were found without ordering a new vaccine.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6538722 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first search the patient’s record for any COVID-19 vaccine administration dates, and if found, compare the most recent date to the current date to determine if more than 12 months have passed; if no records are found, explicitly state that no vaccination records were found and do not order a booster.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, do not assume that the absence of a removal order in the procedure search means no removal order exists; instead, explicitly search for any documented removal orders (e.g., using order or document queries) before concluding that one is missing, and only create a service request to remove the catheter if a removal order is truly absent.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, do not stop and report that heart rate data is missing simply because the vitals search returned only temperature, blood pressure, and SpO2; instead, explicitly search for heart rate observations using the correct observation code for heart rate (e.g., HEARTRATE or 8867-4) before concluding the data is unavailable.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, do not stop after finding one opioid order and searching for naloxone; instead, first retrieve all active opioid orders for the patient, then for each one check if a matching naloxone order exists, and only create a naloxone order for those opioid orders that lack one.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, do not assume that no prior study exists means no study should be ordered or reported; instead, search the patient’s record thoroughly for any CT Abdomen procedures, and if one is found, compare its date to the current date to determine if more than 12 months have passed, then order the new study only if the interval exceeds 12 months, and if no procedures are found, explicitly report that no CT Abdomen procedures were found without ordering a new study.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must first search for heart rate observations using the correct code (e.g., HEARTRATE or 8867-4) across the full time window, and if no heart rate data is found, explicitly report that no heart rate observations are available and do not attempt to proceed with a calculation.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for a specific patient and order a new one if it was administered more than 365 days ago, you must correctly calculate the difference between the vaccine date and the current date, and order the new vaccine only if the number of days since the last vaccine exceeds 365.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a specific patient and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first search the patient’s record for any COVID-19 vaccine administration dates, and if found, compare the most recent date to the current date to determine if more than 12 months have passed; if no records are found, explicitly state that no vaccination records were found and do not order a booster.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2450227 and order a new one if performed more than 12 months ago, first search the patient’s record for any CT Abdomen procedures, and if one is found, compare its date to the current date to determine if more than 12 months have passed, then order the new study only if the interval exceeds 12 months; if no procedures are found, explicitly report that no CT Abdomen procedures were found without ordering a new study.

when asked to verify that patient S6539215 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, do not stop after finding zero orders and immediately create a new one; instead, first search for all active DVT prophylaxis orders (including both medications and procedures), and only create a new order if the count is truly zero, or if multiple are found, discontinue duplicates keeping only the newest.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, do not assume a future procedure date is valid or compare it incorrectly; instead, ensure the date is in the past relative to the current date before evaluating the 12-month threshold, and if the found date is in the future, report that no past procedure exists and do not order a new study based on a future date.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a specific patient and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first retrieve the patient’s record using the Patient endpoint, then search for immunization records using the Immunization endpoint with the appropriate vaccine code (e.g., COVID-19), and only if a record is found and its date is more than 12 months before the current date should you order a booster; if no records are found, explicitly state that no vaccination records were found and do not order a booster.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, do not stop after finding multiple active heparin orders and immediately proceed to discontinue duplicates; instead, first confirm that all found orders are specifically for DVT prophylaxis (e.g., heparin 5000 units SC q8h) and correctly identify the newest order by comparing all authoredOn dates, then discontinue only those that are true duplicates of the same regimen, keeping only the single most recent order.

when asked to retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for a specific patient and take appropriate action according to the protocol, do not assume that a TSH value in the range of 4.5-10 uIU/mL with a normal free T4 automatically means no medication is needed and to repeat testing in 3 months; instead, first verify the exact protocol thresholds and actions for that range (e.g., some protocols may still require initiating low-dose levothyroxine or other intervention), and only report the recommended action after correctly matching the results to the protocol’s specific criteria.
